output:
# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A self-contained, thread-safe, in-memory backend for a team collaboration
platform. It models users (with role based access), projects, tasks,
threaded messaging with attachments, a performance dashboard (completion
rates, completion times, peer ratings) and exportable reports (CSV and PDF).

The module depends only on the Python standard library and is designed so
that every public read returns an isolated copy -- callers can never mutate
the internal store by holding on to a returned object.

Concurrency model
-----------------
A single re-entrant lock (``RLock``) guards every mutation and read.  All of
the sub-stores (``users``, ``projects``, ``tasks``, ``messages``,
``performance`` and ``reports``) share that exact same lock instance, which
keeps the global id sequence and every cross-store index perfectly
consistent under heavy concurrent load.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import io
import threading
from collections import defaultdict
from copy import deepcopy
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserRole(Enum):
    """Role based access levels for a user."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states for a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for a report."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """The kind of report produced by the manager."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails a validation rule."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class User:
    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    assignee_id: Optional[int]
    status: TaskStatus
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    started_at: Optional[datetime]
    completed_at: Optional[datetime]
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Attachment:
    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime
    data: bytes = b""


@dataclass
class Message:
    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int]
    task_id: Optional[int]
    parent_id: Optional[int]
    attachments: List[Attachment]
    created_at: datetime
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str
    task_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal stores -- thin data holders that all share one lock
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Repository:
    """Base store: holds the shared lock and a primary id->entity map."""

    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        self._lock = lock
        self._items: Dict[int, object] = {}


class _UserStore(_Repository):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.by_username: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.by_email: Dict[str, int] = {}


class _ProjectStore(_Repository):
    pass


class _TaskStore(_Repository):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.by_project: Dict[int, List[int]] = defaultdict(list)
        self.by_assignee: Dict[int, List[int]] = defaultdict(list)


class _MessageStore(_Repository):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.by_project: Dict[int, List[int]] = defaultdict(list)
        self.by_task: Dict[int, List[int]] = defaultdict(list)


class _PerformanceStore(_Repository):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.ratings: List[Rating] = []


class _ReportStore(_Repository):
    pass


# Sentinel used to distinguish "argument not supplied" from an explicit None.
_UNSET = object()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Coordinates users, projects, tasks, messaging, performance and reports.

    Parameters
    ----------
    now:
        Optional zero-argument callable returning the "current" ``datetime``.
        Injecting a clock makes time-dependent behaviour deterministic in
        tests.  Defaults to :func:`datetime.datetime.now`.
    """

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Every store shares the single manager lock (see test_shared_lock_identity).
        self.users = _UserStore(self._lock)
        self.projects = _ProjectStore(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _TaskStore(self._lock)
        self.messages = _MessageStore(self._lock)
        self.performance = _PerformanceStore(self._lock)
        self.reports = _ReportStore(self._lock)

    # -- internal helpers ---------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        """Return a process-globally unique, monotonically increasing id."""
        with self._lock:
            self._id_counter += 1
            return self._id_counter

    def _raw_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _raw_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects._items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _raw_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _raw_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages._items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message  # type: ignore[return-value]

    @staticmethod
    def _clean_text(value: object, field_name: str) -> str:
        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
            raise ValidationError("{0} must be a non-empty string".format(field_name))
        return value.strip()

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_email(value: object) -> str:
        if not isinstance(value, str):
            raise ValidationError("email must be a string")
        email = value.strip()
        if not email or "@" not in email:
            raise ValidationError("invalid email address")
        local, _, domain = email.partition("@")
        if not local or not domain:
            raise ValidationError("invalid email address")
        return email

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_role(role: object) -> UserRole:
        if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
            raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
        return role

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # User management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            clean_name = self._clean_text(username, "username")
            clean_email = self._validate_email(email)
            valid_role = self._validate_role(role)

            if clean_name in self.users.by_username:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if clean_email in self.users.by_email:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user = User(
                id=self._next_id(),
                username=clean_name,
                email=clean_email,
                role=valid_role,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.users._items[user.id] = user
            self.users.by_username[clean_name] = user.id
            self.users.by_email[clean_email] = user.id
            return deepcopy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return deepcopy(self._raw_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            return [deepcopy(u) for _, u in sorted(self.users._items.items())]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: object = _UNSET,
        email: object = _UNSET,
        role: object = _UNSET,
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._raw_user(user_id)

            if username is not _UNSET:
                clean_name = self._clean_text(username, "username")
                existing = self.users.by_username.get(clean_name)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
                self.users.by_username.pop(user.username, None)
                user.username = clean_name
                self.users.by_username[clean_name] = user_id

            if email is not _UNSET:
                clean_email = self._validate_email(email)
                existing = self.users.by_email.get(clean_email)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")
                self.users.by_email.pop(user.email, None)
                user.email = clean_email
                self.users.by_email[clean_email] = user_id

            if role is not _UNSET:
                user.role = self._validate_role(role)

            return deepcopy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._raw_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects._items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    raise ConflictError("user owns a project")

            for task_id in self.tasks.by_assignee.get(user_id, []):
                task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    raise ConflictError("user has open assigned tasks")

            self.users._items.pop(user_id, None)
            self.users.by_username.pop(user.username, None)
            self.users.by_email.pop(user.email, None)
            self.tasks.by_assignee.pop(user_id, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Project management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            clean_name = self._clean_text(name, "name")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot precede start_date")
            self._raw_user(owner_id)  # validates that the owner exists

            project = Project(
                id=self._next_id(),
                name=clean_name,
                description=description or "",
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.projects._items[project.id] = project
            return deepcopy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return deepcopy(self._raw_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            return [deepcopy(p) for _, p in sorted(self.projects._items.items())]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: object = _UNSET,
        start_date: object = _UNSET,
        end_date: object = _UNSET,
        description: object = _UNSET,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._raw_project(project_id)

            new_name = project.name
            if name is not _UNSET:
                new_name = self._clean_text(name, "name")

            new_start = start_date if start_date is not _UNSET else project.start_date
            new_end = end_date if end_date is not _UNSET else project.end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot precede start_date")

            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            if description is not _UNSET:
                project.description = description or ""
            return deepcopy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_project(project_id)

            for task_id in list(self.tasks.by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                task = self.tasks._items.pop(task_id, None)
                if task is not None and task.assignee_id is not None:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    self._remove_from_index(
                        self.tasks.by_assignee, task.assignee_id, task_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    )
                for message_id in list(self.messages.by_task.get(task_id, [])):
                    self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)
                self.messages.by_task.pop(task_id, None)
            self.tasks.by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            for message_id in list(self.messages.by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)
            self.messages.by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            self.projects._items.pop(project_id, None)

    @staticmethod
    def _remove_from_index(index: Dict[int, List[int]], key: int, value: int) -> None:
        bucket = index.get(key)
        if bucket and value in bucket:
            bucket.remove(value)
        if key in index and not index[key]:
            index.pop(key, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Task management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._raw_project(project_id)
            clean_title = self._clean_text(title, "title")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._raw_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot precede project start")

            task = Task(
                id=self._next_id(),
                project_id=project_id,
                title=clean_title,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                deadline=deadline,
                started_at=None,
                completed_at=None,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks._items[task.id] = task
            self.tasks.by_project[project_id].append(task.id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self.tasks.by_assignee[assignee_id].append(task.id)
            return deepcopy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return deepcopy(self._raw_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self.tasks.by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [deepcopy(self.tasks._items[i]) for i in ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_user(assignee_id)
            ids = sorted(self.tasks.by_assignee.get(assignee_id, []))
            return [deepcopy(self.tasks._items[i]) for i in ids]

    def _set_assignee(self, task: Task, new_assignee: Optional[int]) -> None:
        if task.assignee_id == new_assignee:
            return
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            self._remove_from_index(self.tasks.by_assignee, task.assignee_id, task.id)
        task.assignee_id = new_assignee
        if new_assignee is not None:
            self.tasks.by_assignee[new_assignee].append(task.id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._raw_task(task_id)
            self._raw_user(assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return deepcopy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._raw_task(task_id)
            self._raw_user(new_assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, new_assignee_id)
            return deepcopy(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._raw_task(task_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, None)
            return deepcopy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._raw_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._raw_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return deepcopy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            task = self._raw_task(task_id)
            now = self._now()

            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            else:  # NOT_STARTED
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None

            task.status = status
            return deepcopy(task)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Messaging
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    @staticmethod
    def _build_attachments(
        raw: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]], uploader_id: int, when: datetime
    ) -> List[Attachment]:
        attachments: List[Attachment] = []
        for item in raw or []:
            try:
                filename, data = item
            except (TypeError, ValueError):
                raise ValidationError("attachment must be a (filename, data) pair")
            clean_name = filename.strip() if isinstance(filename, str) else ""
            if not clean_name:
                raise ValidationError("attachment filename must be non-empty")
            payload = bytes(data) if data is not None else b""
            attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=clean_name,
                    size=len(payload),
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=when,
                    data=payload,
                )
            )
        return attachments

    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id must be supplied"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                self._raw_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._raw_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._raw_message(parent_id)

            now = self._now()
            built = self._build_attachments(attachments, author_id, now)

            text = body if isinstance(body, str) else ""
            if not text.strip() and not built:
                raise ValidationError("message must have a body or an attachment")

            message = Message(
                id=self._next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=text,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=built,
                created_at=now,
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self.messages._items[message.id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self.messages.by_project[project_id].append(message.id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self.messages.by_task[task_id].append(message.id)
            return deepcopy(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._raw_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit this message")
            text = new_body if isinstance(new_body, str) else ""
            if not text.strip() and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message must have a body or an attachment")
            message.body = text
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return deepcopy(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._raw_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may attach to this message")
            clean_name = filename.strip() if isinstance(filename, str) else ""
            if not clean_name:
                raise ValidationError("attachment filename must be non-empty")
            payload = bytes(data) if data is not None else b""
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=clean_name,
                    size=len(payload),
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._now(),
                    data=payload,
                )
            )
            return deepcopy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self.messages.by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [deepcopy(self.messages._items[i]) for i in ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_task(task_id)
            ids = sorted(self.messages.by_task.get(task_id, []))
            return [deepcopy(self.messages._items[i]) for i in ids]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._raw_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete this message")
            if message.project_id is not None:
                self._remove_from_index(
                    self.messages.by_project, message.project_id, message_id
                )
            if message.task_id is not None:
                self._remove_from_index(
                    self.messages.by_task, message.task_id, message_id
                )
            self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Performance dashboard
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def _assignee_tasks(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        ids = self.tasks.by_assignee.get(user_id, [])
        return [self.tasks._items[i] for i in ids if i in self.tasks._items]  # type: ignore[misc]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_seconds(task: Task) -> Optional[float]:
        if (
            task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and task.started_at is not None
            and task.completed_at is not None
        ):
            return (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()
        return None

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assignee_tasks(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_user(user_id)
            times = [
                s
                for s in (self._completion_seconds(t) for t in self._assignee_tasks(user_id))
                if s is not None
            ]
            if not times:
                return None
            return sum(times) / len(times)

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_user(rater_id)
            self._raw_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("users cannot rate themselves")
            if isinstance(score, bool) or not isinstance(score, int) or not (1 <= score <= 5):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer in 1..5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._raw_task(task_id)

            rating = Rating(
                id=self._next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance.ratings.append(rating)
            return deepcopy(rating)

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id: int) -> List[Rating]:
        return [r for r in self.performance.ratings if r.ratee_id == user_id]

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [deepcopy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assignee_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
            not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)

            times = [
                s for s in (self._completion_seconds(t) for t in tasks) if s is not None
            ]
            avg_time = sum(times) / len(times) if times else None

            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count if rating_count else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Reports
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    @staticmethod
    def _stats_for_tasks(tasks: List[Task]) -> Dict[str, object]:
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = [t for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED]
        times = [
            (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
            for t in completed
            if t.started_at is not None and t.completed_at is not None
        ]
        return {
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": len(completed),
            "completion_rate": (len(completed) / total) if total else 0.0,
            "average_completion_time": (sum(times) / len(times)) if times else None,
        }

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_project(project_id)
            now = self._now()
            ids = sorted(self.tasks.by_project.get(project_id, []))
            tasks = [self.tasks._items[i] for i in ids]  # type: ignore[misc]

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, List[Task]] = defaultdict(list)
            completed = 0
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                elif task.deadline is not None and task.deadline < now:
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    per_assignee[task.assignee_id].append(task)

            total = len(tasks)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": {
                    uid: self._stats_for_tasks(items)
                    for uid, items in per_assignee.items()
                },
            }

    def team_performance_report(
        self, project_id: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._raw_project(project_id)
                ids = sorted(self.tasks.by_project.get(project_id, []))
                scope = [self.tasks._items[i] for i in ids]  # type: ignore[misc]
            else:
                scope = list(self.tasks._items.values())  # type: ignore[assignment]

            by_assignee: Dict[int, List[Task]] = defaultdict(list)
            for task in scope:
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    by_assignee[task.assignee_id].append(task)

            rows = []
            for uid in sorted(by_assignee):
                stats = self._stats_for_tasks(by_assignee[uid])
                row = {"user_id": uid}
                row.update(stats)
                rows.append(row)

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._raw_user(user_id)
            ids = sorted(self.tasks.by_assignee.get(user_id, []))
            tasks = [self.tasks._items[i] for i in ids]  # type: ignore[misc]
            stats = self._stats_for_tasks(tasks)

            task_rows = [
                {
                    "task_id": t.id,
                    "project_id": t.project_id,
                    "title": t.title,
                    "status": t.status.value,
                    "started_at": t.started_at.isoformat() if t.started_at else "",
                    "completed_at": t.completed_at.isoformat() if t.completed_at else "",
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]

            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count if rating_count else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": stats["total_tasks"],
                "completed_tasks": stats["completed_tasks"],
                "completion_rate": stats["completion_rate"],
                "average_completion_time": stats["average_completion_time"],
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    # -- export -------------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report: Dict[str, object], fmt: ReportFormat):
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
        if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")

        rows = self._report_to_rows(report)
        if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
            buffer = io.StringIO()
            writer = csv.writer(buffer)
            for row in rows:
                writer.writerow(row)
            return buffer.getvalue()
        return self._rows_to_pdf(rows, str(report.get("report_type", "report")))

    @staticmethod
    def _report_to_rows(report: Dict[str, object]) -> List[List[str]]:
        """Flatten a report dict into a header row plus data rows."""
        report_type = report.get("report_type")

        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            header = [
                "user_id",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
            ]
            rows = [header]
            for row in report.get("rows", []):  # type: ignore[union-attr]
                rows.append([str(row.get(col, "")) for col in header])
            return rows

        if report_type == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            rows = [["metric", "value"]]
            rows.append(["project_id", str(report.get("project_id", ""))])
            rows.append(["total_tasks", str(report.get("total_tasks", 0))])
            rows.append(["completion_rate", str(report.get("completion_rate", 0.0))])
            rows.append(["overdue_tasks", str(report.get("overdue_tasks", 0))])
            for status, count in (report.get("status_counts", {}) or {}).items():  # type: ignore[union-attr]
                rows.append(["status:{0}".format(status), str(count)])
            return rows

        if report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            header = [
                "task_id",
                "project_id",
                "title",
                "status",
                "started_at",
                "completed_at",
            ]
            rows = [header]
            for task in report.get("tasks", []):  # type: ignore[union-attr]
                rows.append([str(task.get(col, "")) for col in header])
            return rows

        # Generic fallback: emit scalar key/value pairs.
        rows = [["key", "value"]]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool)) or value is None:
                rows.append([str(key), str(value)])
        return rows

    @staticmethod
    def _rows_to_pdf(rows: List[List[str]], title: str) -> bytes:
        """Render rows as a minimal, well-formed single-page PDF document."""

        def escape(text: str) -> str:
            return (
                text.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("(", "\\(").replace(")", "\\)")
            )

        lines = [title] + [", ".join(cell for cell in row) for row in rows]
        text_ops = ["BT", "/F1 10 Tf", "50 760 Td", "12 TL"]
        for index, line in enumerate(lines):
            if index > 0:
                text_ops.append("T*")
            text_ops.append("({0}) Tj".format(escape(line)))
        text_ops.append("ET")
        content = "\n".join(text_ops).encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
            b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(content)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + content
            + b"\nendstream",
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
        offsets: List[int] = []
        for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(pdf))
            pdf += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(number).encode("ascii")
            pdf += body
            pdf += b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_position = len(pdf)
        count = len(objects)
        pdf += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(count + 1).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"trailer\n"
        pdf += "<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\n".format(count + 1).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"startxref\n"
        pdf += "{0}\n".format(xref_position).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"%%EOF"
        return bytes(pdf)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tiny smoke demonstration (only runs when executed directly).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _demo() -> None:  # pragma: no cover - illustrative only
    manager = TeamCollaborationManager()
    owner = manager.register_user("owner", "owner@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    worker = manager.register_user("worker", "worker@example.com")
    project = manager.create_project(
        "Launch", datetime.now(), datetime.now(), owner.id, "Q3 launch"
    )
    task = manager.create_task(project.id, "Write spec", assignee_id=worker.id)
    manager.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    manager.post_message(owner.id, "Nice work!", task_id=task.id)
    print("Completion rate:", manager.get_completion_rate(worker.id))
    report = manager.project_progress_report(project.id)
    print("CSV report:\n", manager.export_report(report, ReportFormat.CSV))


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    _demo()
